title: Advanced features
text: spine plot Marimekko chart columns equal width. shifts emphasis marginal proportions conditional proportions, making easier compare segment heights across columns. Set standardize = TRUE:  Compare standard Marimekko see class sizes differ:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     standardize = TRUE   ) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(     title = "Spine plot: equal-width columns",     y = "Conditional proportion"   ) p_base <- ggplot(titanic) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(y = "Proportion")  p1 <- p_base +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   labs(title = "Marimekko (proportional widths)")  p2 <- p_base +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     standardize = TRUE   ) +   labs(title = "Spine (equal widths)")  # Side-by-side (requires patchwork or gridExtra) # p1 + p2 p1 p2

title: Advanced features
text: Pearson residuals measure much cell deviates independence assumption. Positive residuals indicate observations expected; negative residuals indicate fewer. Set residuals = TRUE compute residuals. exposed .resid computed variable, can map aesthetic via after_stat():  can also map residuals colour instead relying fill:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(     aes(       x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq,       alpha = after_stat(abs(.resid))     ),     residuals = TRUE   ) +   scale_x_mekko() +   scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.3, 1), guide = "none") +   labs(title = "Residual shading: stronger opacity = larger deviation") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     residuals = TRUE   ) +   geom_mekko_text(aes(     x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq,     label = after_stat(round(.resid, 1))   ), residuals = TRUE, colour = "white", size = 3) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(title = "Pearson residuals as labels")

title: Advanced features
text: annotate_chisq() runs chi-squared test independence places result (test statistic, degrees freedom, p-value) text annotation:  Customize annotation position appearance:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     residuals = TRUE   ) +   scale_x_mekko() +   annotate_chisq(titanic, Class, Survived, weight = Freq) +   labs(title = "Mosaic plot with chi-squared test") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_mekko() +   annotate_chisq(titanic, Class, Survived,     weight = Freq,     pos_x = 0.5, pos_y = 0.95, hjust = 0.5,     size = 4, fontface = "italic"   )

title: Advanced features
text: small--medium datasets, geom_mekko_jitter() scatters individual data points inside corresponding mosaic tile. row expanded weight, weighted data shows correct number points.  Set seed reproducible jitter placement.
code: ucb <- as.data.frame(UCBAdmissions) ucb_a <- ucb[ucb$Dept == "A", ]  ggplot(ucb_a) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Gender, fill = Admit, weight = Freq),     alpha = 0.3   ) +   geom_mekko_jitter(aes(x = Gender, fill = Admit, weight = Freq),     seed = 42, size = 0.8   ) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(title = "UCB admissions, Dept A: individual applicants")

title: Advanced features
text: geom_mekko_multi() adds third categorical variable (split) subdivides column horizontally stacking fill vertically: Column widths encode x proportions Sub-column widths within column encode conditional split proportions given x Segment heights encode conditional fill proportions given x split  produces richer view faceting three variables share single coordinate space, making relative proportions directly comparable.
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko_multi(aes(     x = Class, split = Sex, fill = Survived, weight = Freq   )) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(title = "Nested mosaic: Class > Sex > Survived")

title: Advanced features
text: default y-axis shows proportions 0 1. scale_y_mekko() replaces fill category labels segment midpoints:  useful want de-emphasize numeric proportions instead label segments directly.
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_mekko() +   scale_y_mekko() +   labs(title = "Fill labels on y-axis")

title: Advanced features
text: fortify_mekko() returns computed tile positions plain data frame without creating plot. useful : Custom downstream analysis Manual ggplot2 layer construction Exporting tile coordinates residuals: returned columns : can use fortified data frame build completely custom plots:

title: Advanced features
text: mekko produces standard ggplot2 layers, can freely combine multiple features:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(     aes(       x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq,       alpha = after_stat(abs(.resid))     ),     residuals = TRUE   ) +   geom_mekko_text(aes(     x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq,     label = after_stat(weight)   ), colour = "white", size = 3.5) +   scale_x_mekko(show_percentages = TRUE) +   scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.4, 1), guide = "none") +   annotate_chisq(titanic, Class, Survived, weight = Freq) +   theme_mekko() +   labs(     title = "Full-featured mosaic plot",     subtitle = "Residual shading + counts + marginal % + chi-squared test",     y = "Proportion"   )

title: Advanced features
text: default, gap controls horizontal (columns) vertical (segments) spacing. Use gap_x gap_y set independently:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     gap_x = 0.04, gap_y = 0   ) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(title = "Wide column gaps, no vertical gaps") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     gap_x = 0, gap_y = 0.03   ) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(title = "No column gaps, visible vertical gaps")

title: Advanced features
text: StatMarimekko computes .tooltip variable containing formatted summary tile (category names, count, proportion). Map text aesthetic use plotly::ggplotly(): .tooltip variable also available via after_stat() custom label construction.
code: library(plotly)  p <- ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(     x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq,     text = after_stat(.tooltip)   )) +   scale_x_mekko() ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")

title: Advanced features
text: Unlike mosaic packages, mekko supports standard ggplot2 tidy evaluation inside aes(). can transform variables inline:
code: ggplot(mtcars) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = factor(cyl), fill = factor(gear))) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(     x = "Cylinders", fill = "Gears",     title = "factor() inside aes() works"   )

title: Advanced features
text: mekko works correctly called :: notation (e.g., mekko::geom_mekko()) without requiring library(mekko). makes safe use inside packages via Imports rather Depends.

title: Getting started with mekko
text: Marimekko (mosaic) plot two-dimensional visualization contingency table. column represents category one variable, segments within column represent categories second variable: - Column widths proportional marginal counts x variable. - Segment heights within column proportional conditional counts fill variable given x. mekko package provides native ggplot2 layer, can combine ggplot2 functionality (facets, themes, annotations, etc.).

title: Getting started with mekko
code: # From CRAN install.packages("mekko")  # From GitHub (when published) devtools::install_github("gogonzo/mekko")

title: Getting started with mekko
text: built-Titanic dataset records survival counts class, sex, age. Let’s visualize survival passenger class.  Three components work: geom_mekko() computes tile positions data. x aesthetic defines columns, fill defines segments, weight provides counts. scale_x_mekko() labels x-axis category names column’s midpoint. Standard ggplot2 functions (labs(), theme(), etc.) work usual.
code: library(ggplot2) library(mekko)  titanic <- as.data.frame(Titanic)  ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(title = "Titanic survival by class", y = "Proportion")

title: Getting started with mekko
text: geom_mekko() understands aesthetics: data already one row per observation (aggregation needed), omit weight:
code: ggplot(mtcars) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = factor(cyl), fill = factor(gear))) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(x = "Cylinders", fill = "Gears")

title: Getting started with mekko
text: gap parameter controls spacing tiles fraction plot area. Default 0.01.  Set gap = 0 seamless mosaic:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     gap = 0.03   ) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(title = "Wider gaps (gap = 0.03)") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     gap = 0   ) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(title = "No gaps")

title: Getting started with mekko
text: scale_x_mekko() can append marginal percentages x-axis labels:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_mekko(show_percentages = TRUE) +   labs(y = "Proportion")

title: Getting started with mekko
text: Use geom_mekko_text() (geom_mekko_label() boxed version) place labels tile centers. label aesthetic can reference computed variables via after_stat(): weight – aggregated count tile cond_prop – conditional proportion within column x_label – x category name fill_label – fill category name  Percentage labels:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   geom_mekko_text(aes(     x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq,     label = after_stat(weight)   ), colour = "white") +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(title = "Counts inside tiles") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   geom_mekko_text(aes(     x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq,     label = after_stat(paste0(round(cond_prop * 100), "%"))   ), colour = "white", size = 3) +   scale_x_mekko()

title: Getting started with mekko
text: theme_mekko() provides clean, minimal theme removes distracting x-axis gridlines:  Since builds theme_minimal(), can override element:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_mekko() +   theme_mekko() +   labs(title = "With theme_mekko()") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_mekko() +   theme_mekko() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Getting started with mekko
text: geom_mekko() supports ggplot2 faceting. panel gets independently proportioned mosaic:
code: ggplot(as.data.frame(Titanic)) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_mekko() +   facet_wrap(~Sex) +   labs(title = "Survival by class, faceted by sex")

title: Getting started with mekko
text: Since fill standard ggplot2 aesthetic, fill scale works:
code: haireye <- as.data.frame(HairEyeColor[, , 1])  ggplot(haireye) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Hair, fill = Eye, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_mekko() +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(title = "Hair vs Eye colour (males)")

title: Getting started with mekko
text: See vignette("advanced-features") spine plots, Pearson residuals, jittered points, three-variable mosaics, programmatic data extraction fortify_mekko().

what: mekko: Marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
title: mekko: Marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
text: Marimekko (mosaic) plots ggplot2. Column widths encode marginal distribution one categorical variable segment heights encode conditional distribution second categorical variable. simpler, lighter alternative ggmosaic.

title: mekko: Marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
code: library(plotly)  p <- ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq,                      text = after_stat(.tooltip))) +   scale_x_mekko() ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")

title: mekko: Marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
code: tiles <- fortify_mekko(titanic, Class, Survived,                            weight = Freq, residuals = TRUE) head(tiles)

title: mekko: Marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
text: mekko extends ggplot2 ggproto system: StatMarimekko computes tile rectangles (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) aggregated weighted counts. Column widths proportional marginal totals; segment heights proportional conditional totals within column. GeomMarimekko inherits GeomRect sensible defaults (white borders, slight transparency). scale_x_mekko() reads tile midpoints stored stat place category labels x-axis. x aesthetic internally remapped x_var ggplot2 treats axis continuous (avoiding discrete/continuous scale conflicts).

title: mekko: Marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
text: mekko designed avoid common pain points ggmosaic: internal ggplot2 API usage – won’t break ggplot2 updates Standard aes() mappings – confusing product() wrapper Works without library() – mekko::geom_mekko() just works tidyr dependency – deprecation warnings Respects factor levels – user-set levels() honored -aes expressions – fill = factor(cyl) works expected Plotly compatible – built-tooltip support via .tooltip Independent x/y gaps – different spacing columns vs segments

title: mekko: Marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
text: ggplot2 (>= 3.5.0) rlang Base R internals (dplyr/tidyr)

code: annotate_chisq(   data,   x,   fill,   weight = NULL,   pos_x = 0.95,   pos_y = 0.05,   size = 3,   hjust = 1,   ... )

title: Annotate a Marimekko plot with a chi-squared test — annotate_chisq
text: data data frame. x Name categorical x variable (unquoted string). fill Name categorical fill variable (unquoted string). weight Name weight variable (unquoted string), NULL unweighted counts. Default NULL. pos_x Numeric x position annotation. Default 0.95. pos_y Numeric y position annotation. Default 0.05. size Text size. Default 3. hjust Horizontal justification. Default 1. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::annotate().

title: Annotate a Marimekko plot with a chi-squared test — annotate_chisq

what: Compute Marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_mekko
title: Compute Marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_mekko
text: Compute Marimekko tile rectangles data frame

title: Compute Marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_mekko
code: fortify_mekko(   data,   x,   fill,   weight = NULL,   gap = 0.01,   gap_x = NULL,   gap_y = NULL,   standardize = FALSE,   residuals = FALSE )

title: Compute Marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_mekko
text: data data frame. x Name categorical x variable (unquoted string). fill Name categorical fill variable (unquoted string). weight Name weight variable (unquoted string), NULL unweighted counts. Default NULL. gap Numeric. Size gap tiles. Default 0.01. gap_x Numeric. Horizontal gap. Overrides gap x. Default NULL. gap_y Numeric. Vertical gap. Overrides gap y. Default NULL. standardize Logical. Equal-width columns. Default FALSE. residuals Logical. Include Pearson residuals. Default FALSE.

title: Compute Marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_mekko
text: data frame columns: x_label, fill_label, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax, x, y, weight, cond_prop, optionally .resid.

title: Compute Marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_mekko
Create a Marimekko (mosaic) plot — StatMarimekko
text: Create Marimekko (mosaic) plot

title: Create a Marimekko (mosaic) plot — StatMarimekko
code: StatMarimekko  geom_mekko(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   stat = "mekko",   position = "identity",   ...,   gap = 0.01,   gap_x = NULL,   gap_y = NULL,   standardize = FALSE,   residuals = FALSE,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )  GeomMarimekko

title: Create a Marimekko (mosaic) plot — StatMarimekko
text: object class StatMarimekko (inherits Stat, ggproto, gg) length 6. object class GeomMarimekko (inherits GeomRect, Geom, ggproto, gg) length 3.

title: Create a Marimekko (mosaic) plot — StatMarimekko
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created ggplot2::aes(). Requires x (categorical) fill (categorical). Optionally accepts weight weighted counts. data data displayed layer. stat statistical transformation use. Defaults "mekko". position Position adjustment. Defaults "identity". ... arguments passed ggplot2::layer(). gap Numeric. Size gap tiles fraction total plot area. Default 0.01. Set 0 gaps. Used horizontal vertical gaps unless gap_x gap_y specified. gap_x Numeric. Horizontal gap columns. Overrides gap x direction. Default NULL (uses gap). gap_y Numeric. Vertical gap segments within columns. Overrides gap y direction. Default NULL (uses gap). standardize Logical. TRUE, columns equal width (spine plot mode). Default FALSE. residuals Logical. TRUE, compute Pearson residuals expose .resid computed variable. Default FALSE. na.rm FALSE (default), removes missing values warning. show.legend Logical. layer included legends? inherit.aes Logical. FALSE, overrides default aesthetics.

title: Create a Marimekko (mosaic) plot — StatMarimekko
text: ggplot2 layer.

title: Create a Marimekko (mosaic) plot — StatMarimekko
code: library(ggplot2)  # Titanic survival by class titanic <- as.data.frame(Titanic) ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_mekko() +   labs(y = "Proportion")   # Hair and Eye color (males) haireye <- as.data.frame(HairEyeColor[, , 1]) ggplot(haireye) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Hair, fill = Eye, weight = Freq), gap = 0.02) +   scale_x_mekko() +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")

what: Jitter individual observations within Marimekko tiles — geom_mekko_jitter
title: Jitter individual observations within Marimekko tiles — geom_mekko_jitter
text: Jitter individual observations within Marimekko tiles

title: Jitter individual observations within Marimekko tiles — geom_mekko_jitter
code: geom_mekko_jitter(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   position = "identity",   ...,   gap = 0.01,   gap_x = NULL,   gap_y = NULL,   standardize = FALSE,   size = 1,   alpha = 0.5,   shape = 16,   colour = "black",   seed = NA,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = FALSE,   inherit.aes = FALSE )  StatMarimekkoJitter

title: Jitter individual observations within Marimekko tiles — geom_mekko_jitter
text: object class StatMarimekkoJitter (inherits Stat, ggproto, gg) length 6.

title: Jitter individual observations within Marimekko tiles — geom_mekko_jitter
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created ggplot2::aes(). Requires x (categorical) fill (categorical). Optionally accepts weight weighted counts. data data displayed layer. position Position adjustment. Defaults "identity". ... arguments passed ggplot2::layer(). gap Numeric. Size gap tiles fraction total plot area. Default 0.01. Set 0 gaps. Used horizontal vertical gaps unless gap_x gap_y specified. gap_x Numeric. Horizontal gap columns. Overrides gap x direction. Default NULL (uses gap). gap_y Numeric. Vertical gap segments within columns. Overrides gap y direction. Default NULL (uses gap). standardize Logical. TRUE, columns equal width (spine plot mode). Default FALSE. size Point size. Default 1. alpha Point transparency. Default 0.5. shape Point shape. Default 16 (filled circle). colour Point colour. Default "black". seed Random seed reproducible jitter. Default NA (different time). na.rm FALSE (default), removes missing values warning. show.legend Logical. layer included legends? inherit.aes Logical. FALSE, overrides default aesthetics.

title: Jitter individual observations within Marimekko tiles — geom_mekko_jitter
text: ggplot2 layer.

title: Jitter individual observations within Marimekko tiles — geom_mekko_jitter
code: library(ggplot2)  # Small dataset: UCBAdmissions Dept A ucb <- as.data.frame(UCBAdmissions) ucb_a <- ucb[ucb$Dept == "A", ] ggplot(ucb_a) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Gender, fill = Admit, weight = Freq)) +   geom_mekko_jitter(aes(x = Gender, fill = Admit, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_mekko()

title: Add labels with background to a Marimekko plot — geom_mekko_label
code: library(ggplot2)  titanic <- as.data.frame(Titanic) ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mekko(aes(x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   geom_mekko_label(aes(     x = Class, fill = Survived, weight = Freq,     label = after_stat(weight)   )) +   scale_x_mekko()

title: Add text labels to a Marimekko plot — geom_mekko_text
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created ggplot2::aes(). Requires x (categorical) fill (categorical). Optionally accepts weight weighted counts. data data displayed layer. position Position adjustment. Defaults "identity". ... arguments passed ggplot2::layer(). gap Numeric. Size gap tiles fraction total plot area. Default 0.01. Set 0 gaps. Used horizontal vertical gaps unless gap_x gap_y specified. gap_x Numeric. Horizontal gap columns. Overrides gap x direction. Default NULL (uses gap). gap_y Numeric. Vertical gap segments within columns. Overrides gap y direction. Default NULL (uses gap). standardize Logical. TRUE, columns equal width (spine plot mode). Default FALSE. residuals Logical. TRUE, compute Pearson residuals expose .resid computed variable. Default FALSE. size Text size. Default 3.5. colour Text colour. Default "black". na.rm FALSE (default), removes missing values warning. show.legend Logical. layer included legends? inherit.aes Logical. FALSE, overrides default aesthetics.
